data.title: Americold announced Q2 2025 results, delivering $0.36 AFFO/share, with revenues at $650.7 million, net income at $1.6 million, and adjusted FFO at $103.6 million. The company outlined future growth plans and adjusted 2025 full-year outlook.
data.description: Americold Realty Trust, a leader in temperature-controlled logistics, reported financial results for Q2 2025, including a decrease in total revenues to $650.7 million, net income of $1.6 million, and Adjusted FFO per share of $0.36. The Company launched three innovative projects and updated its 2025 full-year outlook with an AFFO/share guidance range of $1.39 - $1.45. Global Warehouse segment same store revenues decreased by 1.5%, while Core EBITDA decreased to $159.1 million. The company is focused on cost control and capturing new opportunities amidst challenging demand. Three innovative projects were launched to drive future growth. The webcast and conference call discussed the results and future plans. Total revenues for the second quarter decreased by 1.5% due to lower volumes in the warehouse segment. The Company's 2025 guidance includes various metrics such as warehouse segment non-same store NOI of $7M - $13M and total maintenance capital expenditures of $60M - $70M. The global warehouse segment saw decrease in same store revenue and NOI but an increase in services margin. Non-same store warehouse revenues and NOI were $20,976 and $1,779 respectively. Overall, Americold Realty Trust continues to navigate headwinds and focus on operational efficiency and growth opportunities.

data.title: Cartesian Therapeutics: Q2 cash at $162.1M, Phase 3 trial for Descartes-08 initiated, Phase 2 data expected in 2H25. Cells-based autoimmune therapy in development.
data.description: Cartesian Therapeutics, Inc., a biotechnology company, reported financial results for Q2 of 2025, with $162.1 million in cash, expected to support operations until mid-2027. They initiated the Phase 3 AURORA trial of Descartes-08 in myasthenia gravis and are expecting data from a Phase 2 trial in systemic lupus erythematosus. They also plan to begin a Phase 2 pediatric basket trial of Descartes-08. Descartes-08, an autologous anti-B cell maturation antigen CAR-T therapy, has shown promising results and potential in treating autoimmune diseases. The Company also outlined plans for Descartes-15, a next-generation therapy. Financially, research and development expenses were $14.9 million, net income was $15.9 million, and they have various assets including property, research assets, and investments totaling $388.9 million. For more detailed financial info, visit Benzinga.com.

data.title: Celcuity Inc. (Nasdaq: CELC) to report Q2 2025 financial results on Aug 14, 2025; webcast at 4:30pm ET.
data.description: Celcuity Inc. (Nasdaq: CELC), a company specializing in targeted therapies for oncology, is set to disclose its financial performance for the second quarter of 2025 after markets close on August 14, 2025. This information will be accompanied by a webcast/teleconference at 4:30 p.m. Eastern Time on the same day, during which management will elaborate on the results and deliver a corporate update.

data.title: Appian reveals Q2 2025 financial performance, stay tuned for the numbers.
data.description: A recent report reveals that the unemployment rate in the US has decreased to 4.8% in the month of August. This marks a significant improvement compared to the previous month's rate of 5.4%. The report also highlights that the economy added 235,000 jobs in August, surpassing economists' expectations of 720,000 jobs. Despite concerns over the impact of the Delta variant on economic recovery, these numbers reflect a positive trend in the labor market. The leisure and hospitality sector showed the most growth, adding 74,000 jobs, followed by professional and business services with an increase of 60,000 jobs. These figures suggest a promising outlook for the US economy as it continues to recover from the effects of the pandemic.

data.title: Rural King partners with Anne Wilson for benefit concert in Kentucky on Aug. 11, supporting tornado relief efforts.
data.description: On August 11, all funds generated from an event held at Corbin Arena and its livestream will go towards supporting tornado relief efforts. Rural King, the biggest privately owned farm and home retailer in America, has teamed up with a platinum-certified, Grammy-nominated artist. The event is taking place in Mattoon, Illinois.

data.description: An analyst warns that XRP is likely to remain weak unless whale wallets start receiving daily inflows of over 5 million tokens.

data.title: Harvey expands in Toronto, investing in new hub, hiring locally; focusing on Canadian legal professionals.
data.description: A prominent AI platform for legal and professional services, Harvey, has revealed plans to expand its presence in Canada by launching a new hub in Toronto next month. This move is a strategic component of Harvey's worldwide expansion initiative, showcasing a commitment to the Canadian market by focusing on recruiting local talent in engineering and sales. The company aims to cater to the specific requirements of Canadian legal professionals. With Toronto being renowned for its high-quality engineering expertise, Harvey is seizing the opportunity to tap into this talent pool for sustained growth.

data.title: PENN Entertainment, Inc. announced strong Q2 results with record gaming revenue in online sports betting and online casino.
data.description: In WYOMISSING, Pennsylvania, PENN Entertainment, Inc. (Nasdaq: PENN) released its financial data for the second quarter of 2025. CEO and President Jay Snowden stated that the Company's retail properties performed well during this period. The Interactive segment achieved a milestone with record gaming revenue from online sports betting (OSB) and online casino (iCasino). These results were driven by continuous improvements in products, leading to positive outcomes for the Company.

data.title: Backblaze (BLZE) reports strong Q2 2025 results: B2 revenue up 29%, aiming to be Adjusted Free Cash Flow positive in Q4.
data.description: Backblaze, Inc. revealed their Q2 results for the period ending June 30, 2025. The company, listed as BLZE on Nasdaq, reported promising numbers. B2 revenue saw impressive growth, climbing from 23% to 29% sequentially. The CEO, Gleb Budman, expressed contentment with the performance and emphasized the company's advancement towards achieving Adjusted Free Cash Flow positivity by Q4. Backblaze prides itself on being a cloud storage pioneer providing a contemporary option compared to conventional cloud services.

data.title: Better Home & Finance Holding Company (NASDAQ: BETR; BETRW) reported Q2 2025 results, aiming for Adjusted EBITDA breakeven by Q3 2026.
data.description: In New York, Better Home & Finance Holding Company (NASDAQ: BETR; BETRW), a digitally native homeownership firm, announced its financial performance for the second quarter ending June 30, 2025. The company is determined to pursue expansion regardless of economic or mortgage market conditions and aims to reach Adjusted EBITDA breakeven by Q3 2026. Positioned uniquely to succeed in the current climate, the company is focused on growth and achieving profitability targets.

data.title: SharkNinja reports Q2 2025 results: Net sales up 15.7% to $1.44 billion, net income increased by 105.1% to $139.6 million.
data.description: Needham, Massachusetts-based SharkNinja, Inc. ("SharkNinja" or the "Company") revealed its financial performance for the quarter ending on June 30, 2025. In a comparison with the same period in 2024, the company experienced substantial growth. Net sales saw a significant uptick of 15.7%, reaching $1,444.9 million. Both Gross Margin and Adjusted Gross Margin improved by 90 and 30 basis points, respectively. Net income surged by a remarkable 105.1% to $139.6 million. These positive results reflect the Company's robust performance and potential for future success.

data.title: Altice USA (NYSE: ATUS) reports Q2 2025 results, with improved broadband trends. Dennis Mathew highlights growth in ARPU.
data.description: Altice USA (NYSE: ATUS) announced its second-quarter results for the period ending June 30, 2025. Dennis Mathew, Chairman and CEO of Altice USA, highlighted the company's positive performance. The quarter showed growth in broadband subscribers compared to the previous quarter and the same period last year. Broadband Average Revenue Per User (ARPU) also increased year over year, emphasizing the appeal of their primary services. The company observed advancements throughout its market.

data.title: Natera launches Fetal Focus NIPT for inherited conditions, aiding carriers during partner testing.
data.description: In Austin, Texas, Natera, Inc. (NASDAQ: NTRA), a prominent entity specializing in cell-free DNA (cfDNA) testing and precision medicine, has rolled out Fetal Focus. This is a noninvasive prenatal test (NIPT) tailored for inherited conditions. In scenarios where a pregnant individual is diagnosed as a carrier of a recessive single-gene ailment, it is advised by medical standards to undergo partner testing to gauge the potential risk for the child to be impacted by the condition. This step is essential as it aids in determining the likelihood of the baby inheriting the condition from the carrier parent.
